calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Of Complaint And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar May-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS STRIPE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, amd STRIPE PAYMENTS COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Arbitration Of Complaint And Stay Proceedings.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Stripe, LC and Stripe Payment Company's unopposed Motion To Compel Arbitration Of Complaint And Stay Proceedings is GRANTED.

In Gamboa v. Northeast Community Clinic (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158, the court describes the three-step process to establish the existence of an agreement to arbitrate. The first step requires the party seeking arbitration to carry the initial burden of presenting prima facie evidence of a written agreement to arbitrate the controversy. "A party seeking arbitration can carry its initial burden by attaching a copy of the arbitration agreement purportedly bearing the opposing party's signature. The party seeking arbitration need not follow the normal procedures of document authentication and need only allege the existence of an agreement and support the allegation as provided in rule [3.1330]." (Ramirez v. Charter Communications, Inc. (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 821, 831.) "If that initial burden is met, the second step requires the party opposing arbitration to carry the burden of producing evidence to challenge the authenticity of the agreement. If the opposing party meets the burden of producing sufficient evidence, the third step requires the party seeking arbitration to prove by a preponderance of the evidence that the parties formed a valid contract to arbitrate their dispute." Ramirez v. Golden Queen Mining Co., LLC (2024) 102 Cal.App.5th 821, 830 (cleaned up).) "If the opposing party meets its burden of producing evidence, then in the third step, the moving party must establish with admissible evidence a valid arbitration agreement between the parties. The burden of proving the agreement by a preponderance of the evidence remains with the moving party." (Gamboa, 72 Cal.App.5th at 165-166.)

Defendants carried their initial burden presenting prima facie evidence Plaintiff agreed to arbitrate the controversy. (See Compl., Ex. 1 at sec. 11.4.) The agreement appears to cover the disputes at issue. (See Compl., passim.) Plaintiff has not made any step two showing. Indeed, Plaintiff acknowledges the agreement. Thus, the court finds the parties agreed to arbitrate. Plaintiff has not established a defense to enforcement.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's claims against Defendants are ordered to arbitration and this action is stayed pending the outcome of arbitration proceedings.

The court declines to enter any orders regarding how the arbitration shall proceed.

Moving Defendants are ordered to prepare and submit to Dept. 302 a proposed form of order that quotes verbatim the court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
